content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 19,
1993
Franklin J.
Warren
352 Concord Court
Manteca,
CA
95336
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-93-l68
Dear Mr.
Warren:
You have requested advice regarding your duties as mayor
under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act
(the “Act.”).
1
QUESTION
Under the Act,
may you vote on a proposal to direct staff to
explore options for toxic cleanup assistance at the Diamond
National site?
CONCLUSION
You may not vote on a proposal to direct staff to explore
options for toxic cleanup assistance at the Diamond National site,
unless there will be no financial effect on your property which is
within 300 feet of the site.
FACTS
You are the Mayor of the City of Manteca and also serve as
the chair of the Manteca Redevelopment Agency,
which is comprised
exclusively of the city council members.
You own a commercial building at 116,
118 and 120
W.
Yosemite
Avenue.
You also lease a commercial building at 115
W.
Yosemite
Avenue.
Diamond National owns property approximately
120 feet
south of the real property you own and 335 feet south of the
property you lease.
1
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000 et q.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—93—168
Page
2
The San Joaquin Health Department has determined that the
Diamond National site is contaminated with gasoline,
both in
ground water and soil.
A remediation plan is being prepared for
the property; however,
Diamond National is bankrupt and unable to
pay for the cleanup.
Prospective buyers of the property seek assistance from the
Manteca Redevelopment Agency
(“agency”)
to cleanup the toxic
contamination on the site and to assist in the cost to redevelop
the property.
The agency voted 2-2 to grant permission to agency
staff to explore options for assistance.
Therefore, the lack of
an agency decision leaves the matter unresolved.
You did not
participate in the decision.
Since the matter is deadlocked,
you would like to know if you
may vote only on a proposal to direct staff to explore options for
toxic cleanup assistance at the Diamond National site.
You would
neither vote on any specific staff proposal nor any grant of
monetary assistance.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making,
or otherwise using his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the of
ficial has
a financial interest.
Section 87103 specifies that an
official has a financial interest within the meaning of Section
87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have
a material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on
the public generally,
on the official or a member of his or her
immediate family or on:
Any real property in which the public official
has a direct or indirect interest worth one
thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
Section 87103(b).
Section 87103 specifies that a public official has a
financial interest in a decision only if it is reasonably foresee
able that the decision will have amaterial financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally,
on the
official’s financial interest.
Whether the financial consequences
of a decision are reasonably foreseeable at the time a
governmental decision is made depends on the facts of each
particular case.
An effect is considered reasonably foreseeable
if there is a substantial likelihood that it will occur, certainty
is not required.
However, if an effect is only a mere possibility,
it is not reasonably foreseeable.
(In re Thorner
(1975)
1
FPPC
Ops.
198.)
Regulation 18702.3 defines material financial effect for an
ownership interest in real property which is indirectly involved


File No.
A—93—l68
Page
3
in a decision.
Pursuant to Regulation 18702.3(a), the effect of a
decison is material if:
(1)
The real property in which the official
has an interest,
or any part of that real property,
is located within a
300 foot radius of the
boundaries
(or the proposed boundaries)
of the
property which is the sub:ject of the decision,
unless the decision will have no financial effect
upon the official’s real property interest.
(Emphasis added.)
Since you own property within 300 feet of the Diamond
National site,
you may not participate in making any governmental
decision regarding the site cleanup unless there will be no
financial effect on your property
Therefore,
you must disqualify yourself from voting on a
proposal to direct staff to explore options for assistance
regarding the cleanup of the toxic contamination, unless there
will be no financial effect on your property.
You may,
however, represent your interests before the city in
your private capacity.
Regulation 18700.1(b)
states that an
official is not attempting to use his or her official position to
influence a governmental decision if the official:
(1)
Appears in the same manner as any other
member of the general public before an agency in
the course of its prescribed governmental function
solely to represent himself or herself on a matter
which is related to his or her personal interests.
An official’s “personal interests” include, but are
not limited to:
(A)
An interest in real property which is
wholly owned by the official or members of his or
her immediate family.
*****
Regulation 18700.1(b).
2
“Making a governmental decision” is broadly defined in the Act,
whenever an official:
(1)
Votes on a matter;
(2)
Appoints a
person;
(3)
Obligates or commits his or her agency to any course
of action;
(4)
Enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of
his or her agency;...Regulation 18700(b).


File No.
A—93—168
Page
4
Therefore,
although you may not participate in your official
capacity regarding any governmental decisions regarding the cleanup
of the Diamond National property,
you may appear before the city
council or the agency to represent your personal interests if you
meet the criteria in Regulation 18700.1(b).
I trust this answers your question.
Sincerely,
Jeff Narschner
General Counsel
By:
gal Counsel
JN:JS:ak

QUESTION: Under the Act,
may you vote on a proposal to direct staff to
explore options for toxic cleanup assistance at the Diamond
National site?

CONCLUSION: You may not vote on a proposal to direct staff to explore
options for toxic cleanup assistance at the Diamond National site,
unless there will be no financial effect on your property which is
within 300 feet of the site.
